title: Rules of Hooks and Best Practices
description: Follow the rules to avoid bugs.

slides.title: The Golden Rules of Hooks 👑
slides.content:
# Two Essential Rules

1. **Only call hooks at the top level.** Don't call them inside loops, conditions, or nested functions. This ensures that hooks are called in the same order each render, which is how React preserves state between renders.

2. **Only call hooks from React function components or custom hooks.** Don't call them from regular JavaScript functions.

Violating these rules can lead to subtle bugs where state gets out of order.

slides.title: Correct vs Incorrect
slides.content: Examples of what NOT to do:

quizData.question: It's okay to call useState inside an if statement as long as you're careful.
quizData.explanation: False. Hooks must always be called in the same order, so you cannot conditionally call them. Use conditionals inside the hook or lift the condition outside.
